blocks.content: Ethella remained in the training hall, wearing the same outfit as yesterday.
Her eyebrows twitched as she sat cross-legged without moving a muscle.
Her forehead wrinkled, and her nose bridge twisted.
The veins on her neck bulged as she gritted her teeth, and her body began to shake violently.
Thud thud thud thud thud.
The building shook along with Ethella's body. The walls and ceiling vibrated, sending dust raining down.
Finally, her eyes snapped open, and she exhaled a rough breath before collapsing forward.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Hah! Hah!
Ethella, who had been battling a mind demon since dawn, was drenched in cold sweat.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: What was that?
She couldn't pinpoint it exactly, but it was a type that infiltrated human minds and blocked memories.
Dark magic belonged to the orthodox school, but the malice embedded in the magic was intense enough to push Ethella's vigilance to the extreme.
Had it not been for the laws of the Karsis Diocese, she too would have lost her memories without a fight.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: It's dark magic. But it's strictly forbidden within the school.
Ethella checked the time.
Though it was summer and still bright, it was past the time when school classes would have ended.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Does that mean I was trapped by the mind demon for over 12 hours?

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Considering Ethella's supreme mental strength, it was an incredibly powerful magic.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: What was even more astonishing was that no one had come looking for her despite her skipping classes entirely.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: She wasn't the only one affected.
Once that thought crossed her mind, Ethella rushed out of the training hall without even changing her clothes.
A flash of light passed over the magic school's sky.
Then, as if drawn by a magnet, it curved toward the ground and plummeted.
Without even a sound of impact, a single man landed.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Dammit! Am I too late?
Sade, who had ridden a carriage all night to reach Creas, used spatial teleportation to arrive at the main gate as soon as he entered.
As expected, the guards stood motionless, showing no reaction.
It was dark magic.
And an extremely high-level one at that.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: I need to find the master first.
Sade turned his steps toward the faculty quarters.
As Sade's vigilance triggered, entering the Spirit Zone, Ethella descended in a beam of light.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Teacher Ethella! What's the situation?

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Teacher Ethella! What's the situation?
Sade, who had just returned from a trip, asked as if he already knew about the incident at the school, making Ethella puzzled.
But since every second counted, she cut straight to the point.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: It seems we've fallen victim to dark magic. Everyone—students and teachers—has lost their memories.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Sade had expected it, but facing the reality was still shocking.
No matter how insane, launching a surprise attack on Alpheas Magic School, one of the kingdom's top five prestigious schools, was unthinkable.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: There's no time. First, tell me what's happening.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Scanning with the Spirit Zone, the entire school is heading up the mountain. Judging by the direction, it seems to be toward the Uncrossable Bridge.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: There are three escorts, and three others are tailing them. Among the teachers… it seems I'm the only one who managed to escape.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Arcane's dark magic was at the level of a grand mage, but Ethella was also a bishop of the Karsis Diocese, so he couldn't have dominated her mind.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: That alone was a relief, Sade thought as he explained what he knew.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Let me brief you quickly. The one behind this is Viltor Arcane. A grand mage who threw the world into chaos 40 years ago.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: I've heard of him. Why would such a person target the magic school…?

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Sade bit his lip.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: It was related to the personal history of his master, Alpheas.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: But the only person he could rely on now was Ethella, and given her character, she would accept the truth without prejudice.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Viltor Arcane… is the headmaster's master.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Ethella blinked.
But as Sade expected, she didn't imagine anything beyond what she'd heard.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: I see. But Arcane wasn't among the ones leading the students. What's the situation?

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: We can't. If you're affected by mental magic, it's not easy to break free. Trying to save the students one by one will only backfire.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: But we can't just leave them like this.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: You mentioned three people are tailing them. Do you know who they are?

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: I couldn't confirm exactly… but it might be Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Shirone?

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Hearing Ethella's words, Sade also remembered.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Arcane's magic was likely cast around 24 hours ago.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: The only students who could have been outside the school at that time were Shirone and his group, who were on suspension.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: That's probably correct. If it's them, they'll be fine for now. They're smart kids, so they'll know what to do.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Let's leave it to them for now, and Teacher Ethella, please find Arcane.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: What will you do, Teacher Sade?

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: I'll look for the headmaster. Finding the master and dispelling the dark magic is the priority.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Understood. Then I'll find Arcane.
Sade looked around the mountain ridge.
The school grounds were so vast that he had no idea where Arcane might be.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Hundreds of people were affected simultaneously. A magic of this scale would have drained a significant amount of mental energy.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: He's likely hiding in a secluded place. The mountain is the best place to search.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: How far does your Spirit Zone extend in Jonah Mode?

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Hmm, roughly a diameter of two kilometers.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Sade nodded after a rough calculation.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Marking about five points should suffice. Patrol around the training grounds. I'll head to the school.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Alright. I'll capture Arcane and then save the students.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Don't push yourself too hard. Arcane is…

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Sade swallowed his words.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Telling her how strong he was would only fuel her sense of duty.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: No. I leave it to you.
Feeling the responsibility as Alpheas's disciple, Sade bowed his head on behalf of his master.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: I don't know the details, but I'm also a teacher at this school. I'll do my best for the students.

blocks.speaker: Sade
blocks.content: Yes. Then…
From now on, it was a race against time.
As Sade teleported toward the school, Ethella took a deep breath and focused her mind.
Her sequence formula rapidly ascended, expanding her Spirit Zone.
The unique expansion type exclusive to Jonah unfolded, allowing her to sense everything within a two-kilometer radius through synesthesia.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: First, the 26th Training Ground.
Ethella's body transformed into light as she flew toward the mountain peak.
With a single teleportation, she covered one kilometer.
The Mage's Battle (1)
Canis and his group gathered the memory-blocked people at the starting point of the Uncrossable Bridge.
Arin's face was pale after completing her task.
Even though they were under Abyss Nova, mentally controlling hundreds of people to move was an exhausting ordeal.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Why here of all places? There's just a bridge and a cliff.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: No idea. Maybe they're waiting for someone?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Cliff, mental control, villains. With just these three elements, the answer is obvious.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: They plan to throw them off the cliff.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: What? Why would they do something so insane?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Logically, it makes no sense. There's nothing to gain from killing students.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: But what if it's an emotional issue? Like resentment or revenge.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That makes sense.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Also, if they really planned to do such a thing, waiting like this wasn't the best approach.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We need to act before that happens. We might even have to fight.
For ten minutes, nothing happened.
The students and teachers stood frozen under mental control, while Canis and Arin looked over the cliff, waiting for their master.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Are you sure this is okay, Canis? We searched the entire school but couldn't bring Alpheas.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: We have no choice. The master told us to be especially careful if we encountered Alpheas.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: We just need to do what we can.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Still… I feel like we'll get scolded.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Arin. The master is kind. He'd never scold us for something like this, so don't worry.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: Hey. When is your master showing up? Did he run away or something?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Arin, step back.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: But Canis…

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: You don't need to intervene. Focus on controlling the people. I'll handle this.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Who are you? How did you break free from the mental control?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Dark magic is already outdated, isn't it? There are plenty of ways to counter it.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Nonsense. If it were that easy, the ones behind us wouldn't be affected either.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: True…

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Canis looked up at the sun.
At the mountain peak, the light was still strong.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: But as the disciple of a grand mage, there was no need to negotiate terms with students.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: Hey, what's the plan? This wasn't part of the contract. I can handle it if you double the pay.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: I don't need your help. I'll handle it myself.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: Suit yourself.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: Hurry up and come at me. I'm busy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'll handle this. You guys stop that swordsman.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: That guy? He doesn't seem like he'll interfere.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You never know how things might turn. He's vicious enough to throw Teacher Shina off a cliff.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't believe a word he says.
